front: Philosopher: popularized Naturphilosophie, grounding reality in an original unity of opposites; wrote Ideas for a Philosophy of Nature
Philosopher: Samuel Taylor Coleridge drew heavily on this thinker's Naturphilosophie in Biographia Literaria
Work: traces the 'absolute I' coming to know itself; argues there must be 'one mediating principle in all knowledge' and that art is the 'eternal organ and document of philosophy'
Work: argues evil is the ground that teaches the difference between good and evil and is necessary for perfection
back: Philosophical Inquiries into the Essence of Human Freedom (Schelling)

front: Philosopher: Hegel mocked this former roommate's 'point of absolute indifference' as a 'night in which all cows are black'
Philosopher: characterized early work as a 'negative' project and later sought a 'positive philosophy' beyond the merely logical; lectured on the Philosophy of Mythology at Berlin
